{
  "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/volumebinding/volume_binding.go",
  "newString": "// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "oldString": "// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "originalFile": "/*\nCopyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage volumebinding\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-helpers/storage/ephemeral\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/validation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tstateKey framework.StateKey = Name\n\n\tmaxUtilization = 100\n)\n\n// the state is initialized in PreFilter phase. because we save the pointer in\n// framework.CycleState, in the later phases we don't need to call Write method\n// to update the value\ntype stateData struct {\n\tallBound bool\n\t// podVolumesByNode holds the pod's volume information found in the Filter\n\t// phase for each node\n\t// it's initialized in the PreFilter phase\n\tpodVolumesByNode map[string]*PodVolumes\n\tpodVolumeClaims  *PodVolumeClaims\n\t// hasStaticBindings declares whether the pod contains one or more StaticBinding.\n\t// If not, vloumeBinding will skip score extension point.\n\thasStaticBindings bool\n\tsync.Mutex\n}\n\nfunc (d *stateData) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn d\n}\n\n// VolumeBinding is a plugin that binds pod volumes in scheduling.\n// In the Filter phase, pod binding cache is created for the pod and used in\n// Reserve and PreBind phases.\ntype VolumeBinding struct {\n\tBinder    SchedulerVolumeBinder\n\tPVCLister corelisters.PersistentVolumeClaimLister\n\tscorer    volumeCapacityScorer\n\tfts       feature.Features\n}\n\nvar _ framework.PreFilterPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.FilterPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.ReservePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.PreBindPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.PreScorePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.EnqueueExtensions = &VolumeBinding{}\n\n// Name is the name of the plugin used in Registry and configurations.\nconst Name = names.VolumeBinding\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\tevents := []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t// Pods may fail because of missing or mis-configured storage class\n\t\t// (e.g., allowedTopologies, volumeBindingMode), and hence may become\n\t\t// schedulable upon StorageClass Add or Update events.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.StorageClass, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// We bind PVCs with PVs, so any changes may make the pods schedulable.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolumeClaim, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolume, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// Pods may fail to find available PVs because the node labels do not\n\t\t// match the storage class's allowed topologies or PV's node affinity.\n\t\t// A new or updated node may make pods schedulable.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// A note about UpdateNodeTaint event:\n\t\t// NodeAdd QueueingHint isn't always called because of the internal feature called preCheck.\n\t\t// As a common problematic scenario,\n\t\t// when a node is added but not ready, NodeAdd event is filtered out by preCheck and doesn't arrive.\n\t\t// In such cases, this plugin may miss some events that actually make pods schedulable.\n\t\t// As a workaround, we add UpdateNodeTaint event to catch the case.\n\t\t// We can remove UpdateNodeTaint when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.UpdateNodeLabel | framework.UpdateNodeTaint}},\n\t\t// We rely on CSI node to translate in-tree PV to CSI.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSINode, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// When CSIStorageCapacity is enabled, pods may become schedulable\n\t\t// on CSI driver & storage capacity changes.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIDriver, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIStorageCapacity, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t}\n\treturn events\n}\n\n// podHasPVCs returns 2 values:\n// - the first one to denote if the given \"pod\" has any PVC defined.\n// - the second one to return any error if the requested PVC is illegal.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) podHasPVCs(pod *v1.Pod) (bool, error) {\n\thasPVC := false\n\tfor _, vol := range pod.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tvar pvcName string\n\t\tisEphemeral := false\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase vol.PersistentVolumeClaim != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = vol.PersistentVolumeClaim.ClaimName\n\t\tcase vol.Ephemeral != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = ephemeral.VolumeClaimName(pod, &vol)\n\t\t\tisEphemeral = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Volume is not using a PVC, ignore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\thasPVC = true\n\t\tpvc, err := pl.PVCLister.PersistentVolumeClaims(pod.Namespace).Get(pvcName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// The error usually has already enough context (\"persistentvolumeclaim \"myclaim\" not found\"),\n\t\t\t// but we can do better for generic ephemeral inline volumes where that situation\n\t\t\t// is normal directly after creating a pod.\n\t\t\tif isEphemeral && apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"waiting for ephemeral volume controller to create the persistentvolumeclaim %q\", pvcName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.Status.Phase == v1.ClaimLost {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q bound to non-existent persistentvolume %q\", pvc.Name, pvc.Spec.VolumeName)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q is being deleted\", pvc.Name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif isEphemeral {\n\t\t\tif err := ephemeral.VolumeIsForPod(pod, pvc); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn hasPVC, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point to check if pod has all\n// immediate PVCs bound. If not all immediate PVCs are bound, an\n// UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is returned.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\t// If pod does not reference any PVC, we don't need to do anything.\n\tif hasPVC, err := pl.podHasPVCs(pod); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, err.Error())\n\t} else if !hasPVC {\n\t\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{})\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\tpodVolumeClaims, err := pl.Binder.GetPodVolumeClaims(logger, pod)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif len(podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsImmediate) > 0 {\n\t\t// Return UnschedulableAndUnresolvable error if immediate claims are\n\t\t// not bound. Pod will be moved to active/backoff queues once these\n\t\t// claims are bound by PV controller.\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tstatus.AppendReason(\"pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims\")\n\t\treturn nil, status\n\t}\n\t// Attempt to reduce down the number of nodes to consider in subsequent scheduling stages if pod has bound claims.\n\tvar result *framework.PreFilterResult\n\tif eligibleNodes := pl.Binder.GetEligibleNodes(logger, podVolumeClaims.boundClaims); eligibleNodes != nil {\n\t\tresult = &framework.PreFilterResult{\n\t\t\tNodeNames: eligibleNodes,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{\n\t\tpodVolumesByNode: make(map[string]*PodVolumes),\n\t\tpodVolumeClaims: &PodVolumeClaims{\n\t\t\tboundClaims:                podVolumeClaims.boundClaims,\n\t\t\tunboundClaimsDelayBinding:  podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsDelayBinding,\n\t\t\tunboundVolumesDelayBinding: podVolumeClaims.unboundVolumesDelayBinding,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getStateData(cs *framework.CycleState) (*stateData, error) {\n\tstate, err := cs.Read(stateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\ts, ok := state.(*stateData)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unable to convert state into stateData\")\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n// It evaluates if a pod can fit due to the volumes it requests,\n// for both bound and unbound PVCs.\n//\n// For PVCs that are bound, then it checks that the corresponding PV's node affinity is\n// satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// For PVCs that are unbound, it tries to find available PVs that can satisfy the PVC requirements\n// and that the PV node affinity is satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// If storage capacity tracking is enabled, then enough space has to be available\n// for the node and volumes that still need to be created.\n//\n// The predicate returns true if all bound PVCs have compatible PVs with the node, and if all unbound\n// PVCs can be matched with an available and node-compatible PV.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Filter(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tpodVolumes, reasons, err := pl.Binder.FindPodVolumes(logger, pod, state.podVolumeClaims, node)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(reasons) > 0 {\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tfor _, reason := range reasons {\n\t\t\tstatus.AppendReason(string(reason))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status\n\t}\n\n\t// multiple goroutines call `Filter` on different nodes simultaneously and the `CycleState` may be duplicated, so we must use a local lock here\n\tstate.Lock()\n\tstate.podVolumesByNode[node.Name] = podVolumes\n\tstate.hasStaticBindings = state.hasStaticBindings || (podVolumes != nil && len(podVolumes.StaticBindings) > 0)\n\tstate.Unlock()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreScore invoked at the preScore extension point. It checks whether volumeBinding can skip Score\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tif pl.scorer == nil {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif state.hasStaticBindings {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Score(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tif pl.scorer == nil {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tpodVolumes, ok := state.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\t// group by storage class\n\tclassResources := make(classResourceMap)\n\tfor _, staticBinding := range podVolumes.StaticBindings {\n\t\tclass := staticBinding.StorageClassName()\n\t\tstorageResource := staticBinding.StorageResource()\n\t\tif _, ok := classResources[class]; !ok {\n\t\t\tclassResources[class] = &StorageResource{\n\t\t\t\tRequested: 0,\n\t\t\t\tCapacity:  0,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tclassResources[class].Requested += storageResource.Requested\n\t\tclassResources[class].Capacity += storageResource.Capacity\n\t}\n\treturn pl.scorer(classResources), nil\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Reserve reserves volumes of pod and saves binding status in cycle state.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Reserve(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *framework.Status {\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node will be reserved for the given pod\n\tpodVolumes, ok := state.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif ok {\n\t\tallBound, err := pl.Binder.AssumePodVolumes(klog.FromContext(ctx), pod, nodeName, podVolumes)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstate.allBound = allBound\n\t} else {\n\t\t// may not exist if the pod does not reference any PVC\n\t\tstate.allBound = true\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreBind will make the API update with the assumed bindings and wait until\n// the PV controller has completely finished the binding operation.\n//\n// If binding errors, times out or gets undone, then an error will be returned to\n// retry scheduling.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreBind(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif s.allBound {\n\t\t// no need to bind volumes\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node will be pre-bound for the given pod\n\tpodVolumes, ok := s.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"no pod volumes found for node %q\", nodeName))\n\t}\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Trying to bind volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n\terr = pl.Binder.BindPodVolumes(ctx, pod, podVolumes)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Failed to bind volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"err\", err)\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Success binding volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Unreserve clears assumed PV and PVC cache.\n// It's idempotent, and does nothing if no cache found for the given pod.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Unreserve(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) {\n\ts, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node may be unreserved\n\tpodVolumes, ok := s.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tpl.Binder.RevertAssumedPodVolumes(podVolumes)\n}\n\n// New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc New(ctx context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, fh framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.VolumeBindingArgs)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type VolumeBindingArgs, got %T\", plArgs)\n\t}\n\tif err := validation.ValidateVolumeBindingArgsWithOptions(nil, args, validation.VolumeBindingArgsValidationOptions{\n\t\tAllowVolumeCapacityPriority: fts.EnableVolumeCapacityPriority,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tpodInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().Pods()\n\tnodeInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().Nodes()\n\tpvcInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().PersistentVolumeClaims()\n\tpvInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().PersistentVolumes()\n\tstorageClassInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().StorageClasses()\n\tcsiNodeInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().CSINodes()\n\tcapacityCheck := CapacityCheck{\n\t\tCSIDriverInformer:          fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().CSIDrivers(),\n\t\tCSIStorageCapacityInformer: fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().CSIStorageCapacities(),\n\t}\n\tbinder := NewVolumeBinder(klog.FromContext(ctx), fh.ClientSet(), podInformer, nodeInformer, csiNodeInformer, pvcInformer, pvInformer, storageClassInformer, capacityCheck, time.Duration(args.BindTimeoutSeconds)*time.Second)\n\n\t// build score function\n\tvar scorer volumeCapacityScorer\n\tif fts.EnableVolumeCapacityPriority {\n\t\tshape := make(helper.FunctionShape, 0, len(args.Shape))\n\t\tfor _, point := range args.Shape {\n\t\t\tshape = append(shape, helper.FunctionShapePoint{\n\t\t\t\tUtilization: int64(point.Utilization),\n\t\t\t\tScore:       int64(point.Score) * (framework.MaxNodeScore / config.MaxCustomPriorityScore),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tscorer = buildScorerFunction(shape)\n\t}\n\treturn &VolumeBinding{\n\t\tBinder:    binder,\n\t\tPVCLister: pvcInformer.Lister(),\n\t\tscorer:    scorer,\n\t\tfts:       fts,\n\t}, nil\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [

        " }",

        "-func (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {",
        "+// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.",
        "+func (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {",
        "   return nil",

      ],

      "oldStart": 321

  ],

{

  "newString": "// ScoreNormalizer returns nil.\nfunc (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "oldString": "// ScoreExtensions returns nil.\nfunc (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "originalFile": "/*\nCopyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage framework\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tcorev1helpers \"k8s.io/component-helpers/scheduling/corev1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\textenderv1 \"k8s.io/kube-scheduler/extender/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n\tframeworkruntime \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util\"\n)\n\n// FitPredicate is a function type which is used in fake extender.\ntype FitPredicate func(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status\n\n// PriorityFunc is a function type which is used in fake extender.\ntype PriorityFunc func(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*framework.NodeScoreList, error)\n\n// PriorityConfig is used in fake extender to perform Prioritize function.\ntype PriorityConfig struct {\n\tFunction PriorityFunc\n\tWeight   int64\n}\n\n// ErrorPredicateExtender implements FitPredicate function to always return error status.\nfunc ErrorPredicateExtender(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Error, \"some error\")\n}\n\n// FalsePredicateExtender implements FitPredicate function to always return unschedulable status.\nfunc FalsePredicateExtender(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, fmt.Sprintf(\"pod is unschedulable on the node %q\", node.Node().Name))\n}\n\n// TruePredicateExtender implements FitPredicate function to always return success status.\nfunc TruePredicateExtender(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n}\n\n// Node1PredicateExtender implements FitPredicate function to return true\n// when the given node's name is \"node1\"; otherwise return false.\nfunc Node1PredicateExtender(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tif node.Node().Name == \"node1\" {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, fmt.Sprintf(\"node %q is not allowed\", node.Node().Name))\n}\n\n// Node2PredicateExtender implements FitPredicate function to return true\n// when the given node's name is \"node2\"; otherwise return false.\nfunc Node2PredicateExtender(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tif node.Node().Name == \"node2\" {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, fmt.Sprintf(\"node %q is not allowed\", node.Node().Name))\n}\n\n// ErrorPrioritizerExtender implements PriorityFunc function to always return error.\nfunc ErrorPrioritizerExtender(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*framework.NodeScoreList, error) {\n\treturn &framework.NodeScoreList{}, fmt.Errorf(\"some error\")\n}\n\n// Node1PrioritizerExtender implements PriorityFunc function to give score 10\n// if the given node's name is \"node1\"; otherwise score 1.\nfunc Node1PrioritizerExtender(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*framework.NodeScoreList, error) {\n\tresult := framework.NodeScoreList{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tscore := 1\n\t\tif node.Node().Name == \"node1\" {\n\t\t\tscore = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult = append(result, framework.NodeScore{Name: node.Node().Name, Score: int64(score)})\n\t}\n\treturn &result, nil\n}\n\n// Node2PrioritizerExtender implements PriorityFunc function to give score 10\n// if the given node's name is \"node2\"; otherwise score 1.\nfunc Node2PrioritizerExtender(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*framework.NodeScoreList, error) {\n\tresult := framework.NodeScoreList{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tscore := 1\n\t\tif node.Node().Name == \"node2\" {\n\t\t\tscore = 10\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult = append(result, framework.NodeScore{Name: node.Node().Name, Score: int64(score)})\n\t}\n\treturn &result, nil\n}\n\ntype node2PrioritizerPlugin struct{}\n\n// NewNode2PrioritizerPlugin returns a factory function to build node2PrioritizerPlugin.\nfunc NewNode2PrioritizerPlugin() frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &node2PrioritizerPlugin{}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"Node2Prioritizer\"\n}\n\n// Score return score 100 if the given nodeName is \"node2\"; otherwise return score 10.\nfunc (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) Score(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tscore := 10\n\tif nodeName == \"node2\" {\n\t\tscore = 100\n\t}\n\treturn int64(score), nil\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions returns nil.\nfunc (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// FakeExtender is a data struct which implements the Extender interface.\ntype FakeExtender struct {\n\t// ExtenderName indicates this fake extender's name.\n\t// Note that extender name should be unique.\n\tExtenderName     string\n\tPredicates       []FitPredicate\n\tPrioritizers     []PriorityConfig\n\tWeight           int64\n\tNodeCacheCapable bool\n\tFilteredNodes    []*framework.NodeInfo\n\tUnInterested     bool\n\tIgnorable        bool\n\tBinder           func() error\n\n\t// Cached node information for fake extender\n\tCachedNodeNameToInfo map[string]*framework.NodeInfo\n}\n\nconst defaultFakeExtenderName = \"defaultFakeExtender\"\n\n// Name returns name of the extender.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Name() string {\n\tif f.ExtenderName == \"\" {\n\t\t// If ExtenderName is unset, use default name.\n\t\treturn defaultFakeExtenderName\n\t}\n\treturn f.ExtenderName\n}\n\n// IsIgnorable returns a bool value indicating whether internal errors can be ignored.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsIgnorable() bool {\n\treturn f.Ignorable\n}\n\n// SupportsPreemption returns true indicating the extender supports preemption.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) SupportsPreemption() bool {\n\t// Assume preempt verb is always defined.\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// ProcessPreemption implements the extender preempt function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) ProcessPreemption(\n\tpod *v1.Pod,\n\tnodeNameToVictims map[string]*extenderv1.Victims,\n\tnodeInfos framework.NodeInfoLister,\n) (map[string]*extenderv1.Victims, error) {\n\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy := map[string]*extenderv1.Victims{}\n\t// We don't want to change the original nodeNameToVictims\n\tfor k, v := range nodeNameToVictims {\n\t\t// In real world implementation, extender's user should have their own way to get node object\n\t\t// by name if needed (e.g. query kube-apiserver etc).\n\t\t//\n\t\t// For test purpose, we just use node from parameters directly.\n\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[k] = v\n\t}\n\n\t// If Extender.ProcessPreemption ever gets extended with a context parameter, then the logger should be retrieved from that.\n\t// Now, in order not to modify the Extender interface, we get the logger from klog.TODO()\n\tlogger := klog.TODO()\n\tfor nodeName, victims := range nodeNameToVictimsCopy {\n\t\t// Try to do preemption on extender side.\n\t\tnodeInfo, _ := nodeInfos.Get(nodeName)\n\t\textenderVictimPods, extenderPDBViolations, fits, err := f.selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger, pod, nodeInfo)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If it's unfit after extender's preemption, this node is unresolvable by preemption overall,\n\t\t// let's remove it from potential preemption nodes.\n\t\tif !fits {\n\t\t\tdelete(nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nodeName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Append new victims to original victims\n\t\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[nodeName].Pods = append(victims.Pods, extenderVictimPods...)\n\t\t\tnodeNameToVictimsCopy[nodeName].NumPDBViolations = victims.NumPDBViolations + int64(extenderPDBViolations)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nil\n}\n\n// selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender checks the given nodes->pods map with predicates on extender's side.\n// Returns:\n// 1. More victim pods (if any) amended by preemption phase of extender.\n// 2. Number of violating victim (used to calculate PDB).\n// 3. Fits or not after preemption phase on extender's side.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) ([]*v1.Pod, int, bool, error) {\n\t// If a extender support preemption but have no cached node info, let's run filter to make sure\n\t// default scheduler's decision still stand with given pod and node.\n\tif !f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\terr := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif err.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn []*v1.Pod{}, 0, true, nil\n\t\t} else if err.IsRejected() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err.AsError()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Otherwise, as a extender support preemption and have cached node info, we will assume cachedNodeNameToInfo is available\n\t// and get cached node info by given node name.\n\tnodeInfoCopy := f.CachedNodeNameToInfo[node.Node().Name].Snapshot()\n\n\tvar potentialVictims []*v1.Pod\n\n\tremovePod := func(rp *v1.Pod) error {\n\t\treturn nodeInfoCopy.RemovePod(logger, rp)\n\t}\n\taddPod := func(ap *v1.Pod) {\n\t\tnodeInfoCopy.AddPod(ap)\n\t}\n\t// As the first step, remove all the lower priority pods from the node and\n\t// check if the given pod can be scheduled.\n\tpodPriority := corev1helpers.PodPriority(pod)\n\tfor _, p := range nodeInfoCopy.Pods {\n\t\tif corev1helpers.PodPriority(p.Pod) < podPriority {\n\t\t\tpotentialVictims = append(potentialVictims, p.Pod)\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p.Pod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsort.Slice(potentialVictims, func(i, j int) bool { return util.MoreImportantPod(potentialVictims[i], potentialVictims[j]) })\n\n\t// If the new pod does not fit after removing all the lower priority pods,\n\t// we are almost done and this node is not suitable for preemption.\n\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t// pass\n\t} else if status.IsRejected() {\n\t\t// does not fit\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t} else {\n\t\t// internal errors\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, status.AsError()\n\t}\n\n\tvar victims []*v1.Pod\n\n\t// TODO(harry): handle PDBs in the future.\n\tnumViolatingVictim := 0\n\n\treprievePod := func(p *v1.Pod) bool {\n\t\taddPod(p)\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvictims = append(victims, p)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status.IsSuccess()\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, assume all potential victims to be non-violating.\n\t// Now we try to reprieve non-violating victims.\n\tfor _, p := range potentialVictims {\n\t\treprievePod(p)\n\t}\n\n\treturn victims, numViolatingVictim, true, nil\n}\n\n// runPredicate run predicates of extender one by one for given pod and node.\n// Returns: fits or not.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) runPredicate(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tfor _, predicate := range f.Predicates {\n\t\tstatus := predicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn status\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n}\n\n// Filter implements the extender Filter function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Filter(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) ([]*framework.NodeInfo, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, error) {\n\tvar filtered []*framework.NodeInfo\n\tfailedNodesMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfailedAndUnresolvableMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, node)\n\t\t} else if status.Code() == framework.Unschedulable {\n\t\t\tfailedNodesMap[node.Node().Name] = fmt.Sprintf(\"FakeExtender: node %q failed\", node.Node().Name)\n\t\t} else if status.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n\t\t\tfailedAndUnresolvableMap[node.Node().Name] = fmt.Sprintf(\"FakeExtender: node %q failed and unresolvable\", node.Node().Name)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil, nil, status.AsError()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tf.FilteredNodes = filtered\n\tif f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n\t}\n\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n}\n\n// Prioritize implements the extender Prioritize function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Prioritize(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*extenderv1.HostPriorityList, int64, error) {\n\tresult := extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}\n\tcombinedScores := map[string]int64{}\n\tfor _, prioritizer := range f.Prioritizers {\n\t\tweight := prioritizer.Weight\n\t\tif weight == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tpriorityFunc := prioritizer.Function\n\t\tprioritizedList, err := priorityFunc(pod, nodes)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn &extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, hostEntry := range *prioritizedList {\n\t\t\tcombinedScores[hostEntry.Name] += hostEntry.Score * weight\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor host, score := range combinedScores {\n\t\tresult = append(result, extenderv1.HostPriority{Host: host, Score: score})\n\t}\n\treturn &result, f.Weight, nil\n}\n\n// Bind implements the extender Bind function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Bind(binding *v1.Binding) error {\n\tif f.Binder != nil {\n\t\treturn f.Binder()\n\t}\n\tif len(f.FilteredNodes) != 0 {\n\t\tfor _, node := range f.FilteredNodes {\n\t\t\tif node.Node().Name == binding.Target.Name {\n\t\t\t\tf.FilteredNodes = nil\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"Node %v not in filtered nodes %v\", binding.Target.Name, f.FilteredNodes)\n\t\tf.FilteredNodes = nil\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// IsBinder returns true indicating the extender implements the Binder function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsBinder() bool {\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// IsPrioritizer returns true if there are any prioritizers.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsPrioritizer() bool {\n\treturn len(f.Prioritizers) > 0\n}\n\n// IsFilter returns true if there are any filters.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsFilter() bool {\n\treturn len(f.Predicates) > 0\n}\n\n// IsInterested returns a bool indicating whether this extender is interested in this Pod.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsInterested(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\treturn !f.UnInterested\n}\n\nvar _ framework.Extender = &FakeExtender{}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   return int64(score), nil",
        " }",

        "+func (pl *node2PrioritizerPlugin) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/testing/framework/fake_plugins.go",
  "newString": "func (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "oldString": "func (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}",
  "originalFile": "/*\nCopyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage framework\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n\tframeworkruntime \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/runtime\"\n)\n\n// ErrReasonFake is a fake error message denotes the filter function errored.\nconst ErrReasonFake = \"Nodes failed the fake plugin\"\n\n// FalseFilterPlugin is a filter plugin which always return Unschedulable when Filter function is called.\ntype FalseFilterPlugin struct{}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FalseFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FalseFilter\"\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\nfunc (pl *FalseFilterPlugin) Filter(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, ErrReasonFake)\n}\n\n// NewFalseFilterPlugin initializes a FalseFilterPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFalseFilterPlugin(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &FalseFilterPlugin{}, nil\n}\n\n// TrueFilterPlugin is a filter plugin which always return Success when Filter function is called.\ntype TrueFilterPlugin struct{}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *TrueFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"TrueFilter\"\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\nfunc (pl *TrueFilterPlugin) Filter(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewTrueFilterPlugin initializes a TrueFilterPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewTrueFilterPlugin(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &TrueFilterPlugin{}, nil\n}\n\ntype FakePreFilterAndFilterPlugin struct {\n\t*FakePreFilterPlugin\n\t*FakeFilterPlugin\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl FakePreFilterAndFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FakePreFilterAndFilterPlugin\"\n}\n\n// FakeFilterPlugin is a test filter plugin to record how many times its Filter() function have\n// been called, and it returns different 'Code' depending on its internal 'failedNodeReturnCodeMap'.\ntype FakeFilterPlugin struct {\n\tNumFilterCalled         int32\n\tFailedNodeReturnCodeMap map[string]framework.Code\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakeFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FakeFilter\"\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakeFilterPlugin) Filter(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tatomic.AddInt32(&pl.NumFilterCalled, 1)\n\n\tif returnCode, ok := pl.FailedNodeReturnCodeMap[nodeInfo.Node().Name]; ok {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(returnCode, fmt.Sprintf(\"injecting failure for pod %v\", pod.Name))\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewFakeFilterPlugin initializes a fakeFilterPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFakeFilterPlugin(failedNodeReturnCodeMap map[string]framework.Code) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakeFilterPlugin{\n\t\t\tFailedNodeReturnCodeMap: failedNodeReturnCodeMap,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchFilterPlugin is a filter plugin which return Success when the evaluated pod and node\n// have the same name; otherwise return Unschedulable.\ntype MatchFilterPlugin struct{}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *MatchFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"MatchFilter\"\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\nfunc (pl *MatchFilterPlugin) Filter(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Error, \"node not found\")\n\t}\n\tif pod.Name == node.Name {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, ErrReasonFake)\n}\n\n// NewMatchFilterPlugin initializes a MatchFilterPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewMatchFilterPlugin(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &MatchFilterPlugin{}, nil\n}\n\n// FakePreFilterPlugin is a test filter plugin.\ntype FakePreFilterPlugin struct {\n\tResult *framework.PreFilterResult\n\tStatus *framework.Status\n\tname   string\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakePreFilterPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn pl.name\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the PreFilter extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakePreFilterPlugin) PreFilter(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\treturn pl.Result, pl.Status\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions no extensions implemented by this plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakePreFilterPlugin) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewFakePreFilterPlugin initializes a fakePreFilterPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFakePreFilterPlugin(name string, result *framework.PreFilterResult, status *framework.Status) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakePreFilterPlugin{\n\t\t\tResult: result,\n\t\t\tStatus: status,\n\t\t\tname:   name,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// FakeReservePlugin is a test reserve plugin.\ntype FakeReservePlugin struct {\n\tStatus *framework.Status\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakeReservePlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FakeReserve\"\n}\n\n// Reserve invoked at the Reserve extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakeReservePlugin) Reserve(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, _ string) *framework.Status {\n\treturn pl.Status\n}\n\n// Unreserve invoked at the Unreserve extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakeReservePlugin) Unreserve(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, _ string) {\n}\n\n// NewFakeReservePlugin initializes a fakeReservePlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFakeReservePlugin(status *framework.Status) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakeReservePlugin{\n\t\t\tStatus: status,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// FakePreBindPlugin is a test prebind plugin.\ntype FakePreBindPlugin struct {\n\tStatus *framework.Status\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakePreBindPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FakePreBind\"\n}\n\n// PreBind invoked at the PreBind extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakePreBindPlugin) PreBind(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, _ string) *framework.Status {\n\treturn pl.Status\n}\n\n// NewFakePreBindPlugin initializes a fakePreBindPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFakePreBindPlugin(status *framework.Status) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakePreBindPlugin{\n\t\t\tStatus: status,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// FakePermitPlugin is a test permit plugin.\ntype FakePermitPlugin struct {\n\tStatus  *framework.Status\n\tTimeout time.Duration\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakePermitPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"FakePermit\"\n}\n\n// Permit invoked at the Permit extension point.\nfunc (pl *FakePermitPlugin) Permit(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, _ string) (*framework.Status, time.Duration) {\n\treturn pl.Status, pl.Timeout\n}\n\n// NewFakePermitPlugin initializes a fakePermitPlugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFakePermitPlugin(status *framework.Status, timeout time.Duration) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakePermitPlugin{\n\t\t\tStatus:  status,\n\t\t\tTimeout: timeout,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\ntype FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin struct {\n\tname           string\n\tscore          int64\n\tpreScoreStatus *framework.Status\n\tscoreStatus    *framework.Status\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin.\nfunc (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn pl.name\n}\n\nfunc (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\treturn pl.score, pl.scoreStatus\n}\n\nfunc (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (pl *FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin) PreScore(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\treturn pl.preScoreStatus\n}\n\nfunc NewFakePreScoreAndScorePlugin(name string, score int64, preScoreStatus, scoreStatus *framework.Status) frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin{\n\t\t\tname:           name,\n\t\t\tscore:          score,\n\t\t\tpreScoreStatus: preScoreStatus,\n\t\t\tscoreStatus:    scoreStatus,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewEqualPrioritizerPlugin returns a factory function to build equalPrioritizerPlugin.\nfunc NewEqualPrioritizerPlugin() frameworkruntime.PluginFactory {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, _ framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\t\treturn &FakePreScoreAndScorePlugin{\n\t\t\tname:  \"EqualPrioritizerPlugin\",\n\t\t\tscore: 1,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n}\n",
